claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch API/schema supports WebFetch/WebSearch as external evidence (scope=external) and maintains separation from project-scoped facts (scope=project with required path locators). External research tools are available when agent_tool_profile includes 'research', and fetched/web records must be marked as scope=external without being used as target-project facts.

evidence.why: Core evidence scope enumeration supporting external as a distinct category from project

evidence.summary: Agent prompt conditional: toolProfile.includes('research') explicitly allows WebFetch/WebSearch for external research with instruction to mark those records as scope=external and not use them as project facts
evidence.why: Primary evidence that WebFetch/WebSearch are allowed for external research when agent_tool_profile='research'

evidence.summary: Evidence schema definition requires path for project scope and allows url for external scope, with scope field accepting 'project | external | user | unknown'
evidence.why: Schema proves external scope is structurally supported with URL field vs path field for project scope

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeErrors function validates that every evidence record declares scope as one of the four valid values; missing or invalid scope becomes an error
evidence.why: Evidence scope enforcement prevents missing scope declarations

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentResult blocks results with missing scope by setting status='blocked', recommendation='block', and error_code='EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED'
evidence.why: Runtime enforcement that evidence without valid scope cannot complete successfully

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope function normalizes various scope aliases including 'external', 'web', 'internet', 'research' to 'external', and source types like 'external_web', 'webfetch', 'websearch' to external
evidence.why: Scope normalization supports multiple ways to declare external evidence including WebFetch/WebSearch sources

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues function generates BLOCKED status when project facts lack project-scoped evidence with path locators, with error codes PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED or PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE
evidence.why: Enforcement that existing-project facts require project evidence, not external/user/unknown only

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION field evidence states 'scope is project | external | user | unknown; project facts require project-scoped evidence'
evidence.why: Schema contract explicitly separates project facts from external evidence

evidence.summary: Doctrine states: 'external evidence can come from WebFetch/WebSearch or external docs, but only as background research, cannot alone prove target project existing fields/API/state machine/data flow'
evidence.why: Product doctrine confirms external evidence is allowed but not sufficient for project facts

evidence.summary: Unit test 'claude research profile labels research while keeping tools available' confirms agent_tool_profile='research' sets agent_tool_profile to 'research' and tools remain available
evidence.why: Test evidence proving research profile enables WebFetch/WebSearch access while maintaining tool availability

evidence.summary: Recent explorer agent result with 14 evidence records all using scope='project' with exact paths, demonstrating proper project evidence structure in practice
evidence.why: Real-world example of evidence agents properly structuring project-scoped evidence with path locators

evidence.summary: executionConfig function normalizes agent_tool_profile: 'research', 'fetch', 'web', 'web-research', 'external-research' all map to 'research' for Claude provider
evidence.why: Profile normalization shows multiple alias inputs all enable research mode with external tool access

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION requires evidence field with scope attribute and states 'project facts require project-scoped evidence'
evidence.why: Schema contract explicitly requires evidence records to declare scope and separates project facts from external evidence

assumptions: WebFetch and WebSearch tools are actually available in the agent environment when agent_tool_profile='research' is configured
Agents can successfully fetch from https://example.com or other URLs when the tools are enabled
The boundary snapshot mechanism does not distinguish between project mutations and external fetch cache artifacts
External evidence records would actually pass scope validation when marked as scope='external' with url field
risks: If WebFetch/WebSearch tools are not actually available in the agent environment despite prompt saying they may be available, external evidence gathering would fail
If external fetch operations create cache files in the project directory that are detected by boundary snapshot, legitimate external research could be blocked as mutations
If agents misinterpret the scope field and mark external fetch results as scope='project', the evidence would pass validation but violate the separation principle
If the research profile normalization in evidence-dispatch.ts:447-459 does not match actual Claude Code tool availability, agents may not have the external research tools they expect
missing: Actual execution evidence of WebFetch or WebSearch being used in a demand evidence dispatch with scope='external' records
Test cases specifically validating external evidence scope handling with URL fields
Documentation or examples showing proper structure of external evidence records with url field populated
Evidence that boundary snapshot mechanism correctly ignores or allows external fetch cache artifacts
Confirmation that https://example.com or any specific external URL can be successfully fetched during evidence dispatch

result.notes: Claim verified from code implementation and schema: YOLO demand evidence dispatch supports WebFetch/WebSearch as external evidence with proper scope separation. Key evidence: (1) VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES includes 'external' (evidence-dispatch.ts:16), (2) prompt explicitly allows WebFetch/WebSearch for research profile and requires marking as scope=external (line 273), (3) schema supports url field for external scope vs path field for project scope (lines 305-312), (4) scope validation enforces declarations (lines 150-157, 403-416), (5) projectEvidenceBlockingIssues prevents external-only evidence for project facts (router.ts:513-529), (6) doctrine confirms external evidence allowed as background research (yolo-demand-doctrine.md:70). Missing items are operational validation (actual usage examples, fetch cache handling) not structural support. The implementation correctly separates external research (scope=external with url) from project facts (scope=project with path), satisfying the acceptance criteria.
